title: bistorta affinis
intro: Bistorta affinis, also known as alpine bistort or European bistort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Polygonaceae family, characterized by its pink or white flowers and edible roots. Traditionally, the roots and rhizomes of the bistorta affinis plant have been used in herbal medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The health benefits of bistorta affinis include its ability to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and ulcers, as well as its potential to reduce swelling and alleviate skin irritations. Bistorta affinis can be prepared in various forms, including decoctions, infusions, and dried extracts, which can be used to create teas, tinctures, and salves for medicinal purposes.
